title: HTTPS, TLS Identity, Certificate Trust, Proxies, and Downgrade Boundaries · Automation and Data Client Systems · Program Defense
summary: A production data release board evaluating a changed source contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for https, tls identity, certificate trust, proxies, and downgrade boundaries, producing transport trust and certificate evidence record. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers transport trust and certificate evidence record. Learners progress th…
objectives: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Preserve hostname, chain, validity, algorithm, and revocation-policy checks without disabling verification. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Add an intended private trust root or test certificate without replacing or weakening unrelated trust. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Https Identity · changed case · Quiz Https Identity
steps.instruction:
quiz case — production data release candidate connects to api5.internal.test through proxy.example.test using a private CA and optional client certificate. Verify requested identity, connected endpoint, roots, proxy authority, downgrade policy, and what evidence the browser lab cannot supply. Competency probe: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
